name: Trail of the Viewpoints
name_original: Recorrido circular costero oriental desde Muros de Nalón
description: Hike the Eastern coastal circular route from Muros de Nalón for moderate coastal views, beaches, and viewpoints over 5.6 miles.
region_intro: Hiking around Muros De Nalón offers diverse landscapes, from dramatic coastlines with towering cliffs to picturesque beaches and the Nalón River estuary. The region features well-maintained trails that often pass through pine and eucalyptus forests. This coastal municipality in Asturias, Spain, provides a blend of sea views, riverine scenery, and wooded paths. Its varied terrain includes accessible coastal paths and routes that are part of the historic Camino de Santiago. Best hiking trails around Muros De Nalón The most popular hiking route is Camino del Norte y Paseo de los Miradores — circular desde Muros de Nalón, a 9.2 miles (14.9 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete, combining a section of the Camino de Santiago with the scenic Path of the Viewpoints. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trail of the Viewpoints, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This route follows the "Senda de los Miradores," offering continuous panoramic vistas of the Cantabrian Sea from various natural observatories. Local hikers also love the Western coastal circular route from Muros de Nalón, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through coastal paths and past beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Muros De Nalón is defined by its dramatic cliffs, sandy beaches, and the Nalón River estuary. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Muros De Nalón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Muros De Nalón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Muros de Nalón?
A: Muros de Nalón offers a diverse network of over 25 hiking routes. These trails cater to various preferences, ranging from easy coastal strolls to more moderate paths that explore the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Muros de Nalón?
A: Hiking in Muros de Nalón features a mix of dramatic coastlines with towering cliffs, sandy beaches, and the Nalón River estuary. Trails often pass through pine and eucalyptus forests, providing a blend of sea views, riverine scenery, and wooded paths. You'll find well-maintained paths, including cobbled sections along the coast.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Muros de Nalón has several easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343945521/western-coastal-circular-route-from-muros-de-nalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Western coastal circular route from Muros de Nalón&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.0-mile (6.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 50 minutes, leading through coastal paths and past beaches.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Muros de Nalón?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Muros de Nalón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048071336/camino-del-norte-y-paseo-de-los-miradores-circular-desde-muros-de-nalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Norte y Paseo de los Miradores — circular desde Muros de Nalón&lt;/a&gt;, which combines a section of the Camino de Santiago with the scenic Path of the Viewpoints.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Muros de Nalón?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Muros de Nalón, especially on the coastal paths and through the forests. It's always advisable to keep them on a leash, particularly in areas with wildlife or near cliffs, and to respect local regulations regarding beaches or protected areas.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or scenic spots to discover while hiking?
A: The region is renowned for its spectacular viewpoints. The "Senda de los Miradores" (Path of the Viewpoints) offers continuous panoramic vistas of the Cantabrian Sea. Notable spots include the &lt;strong&gt;Mirador del Espíritu Santo&lt;/strong&gt;, which provides magical ocean views, and the &lt;strong&gt;Atalaya Viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Los Glayos Viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt;, all offering impressive coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Muros de Nalón offers a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. Near the Mirador del Espíritu Santo, you can find the &lt;strong&gt;Castro del Campón&lt;/strong&gt;, an archaeological site. The nearby town of San Esteban de Pravia, part of the municipality, has a historically significant port and a riverside promenade. You can also explore attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/426621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta de Selgas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Muros de Nalón?
A: Muros de Nalón is pleasant for hiking for much of the year. Spring and early autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for coastal walks and forest trails. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, making coastal breezes particularly welcome. Even winter can be suitable for hiking, with fewer crowds and dramatic sea views, provided you dress appropriately.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Muros de Nalón?
A: While Muros de Nalón is a small municipality, local bus services connect it to larger towns in Asturias, which may provide access to some trailheads. However, for maximum flexibility and to reach more remote starting points, driving is often the most convenient option. Many popular routes, like those starting from Muros de Nalón town or San Esteban de Pravia, are accessible.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a hike in Muros de Nalón?
A: Parking is generally available in the main towns and villages, such as Muros de Nalón itself and San Esteban de Pravia. For popular coastal paths like the Senda de los Miradores, designated parking areas are often provided near key access points or viewpoints. Always check local signage for any parking restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Muros de Nalón?
A: The hiking trails in Muros de Nalón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the well-maintained paths, and the diversity of landscapes, from dramatic cliffs to peaceful forests and beautiful beaches.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking opportunities, such as sections of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Muros de Nalón is part of the Northern Route of the Camino de Santiago, offering opportunities for long-distance hiking. You can experience a section of this historic pilgrimage route, often combined with local scenic paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048071336/camino-del-norte-y-paseo-de-los-miradores-circular-desde-muros-de-nalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Norte y Paseo de los Miradores — circular desde Muros de Nalón&lt;/a&gt; is an example that incorporates a part of the Camino.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature beaches or coastal access?
A: Muros de Nalón is famous for its beautiful beaches and coastal trails. Many hikes, especially those along the "Senda de los Miradores," offer continuous views of the Cantabrian Sea and pass by or provide access to beaches like Aguilar Beach, Xilo Beach, and Veneiro Beach. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343955548/trail-of-the-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for coastal scenery.
tips: It is a small cove of fine grijo, located in the shelter of the breakwater of the port of San Esteban and the cliffs of the coastal plain. San Esteban beach has two more rocky areas, known as Focarón and Garruncho, which are only accessible at low tide.

The Mirador del Espíritu Santo receives this name from the Hermitage that is found in it. This viewpoint offers panoramic views from where you can see Cabo de Vidio, the mouth of the Nalón River or the nearby town of San Juan de La Arena.
Approximately one kilometer from the viewpoint of the Holy Spirit is the viewpoint of the Watchtower with views that are worth contemplating.
Mirador de Los Glayos is situated west of San Esteban de Pravia, east of Playa de Aguilar. It overlooks the beaches of Las Llanas and La Cazonera.
The church marks the center of the village, here you will find some bars and small markets where you can provide food.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, so you can start from various points in Muros de Nalón. Parking is generally available in the town. The trail is well-signposted, making it easy to find the starting points.

faqs.question: Is this coastal route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially near cliffs, populated areas, and to protect local wildlife. Be mindful of other hikers and any specific local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer and more crowded. The trail's varied terrain and covered sections make it accessible in different weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Eastern coastal circular route from Muros de Nalón. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How difficult is this hike, and is it suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this hike as moderate, meaning good fitness is required but paths are easily accessible. Other sources describe it as easy to moderate and suitable for all ages, including families. The most challenging part is climbing the 420 steps from the port of San Esteban to the Holy Spirit Viewpoint, but the rest of the path is generally well-prepared and signposted.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 9 kilometers long and typically takes around 2.5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views. Allow extra time for breaks and exploring the various viewpoints.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Eastern coastal circular route from Muros de Nalón?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and generally well-maintained. You'll encounter cobbled paths along the coast, sections through pine and eucalyptus forests, and sandy areas on beaches like El Garruncho Beach. The most notable feature is the 420 steps near the port of San Esteban.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: True to its name, the 'Coastal Path of the Viewpoints' offers numerous stunning vistas. Highlights include Holy Spirit Viewpoint, Atalaya Viewpoint, and Los Glayos Viewpoint. You'll also pass by El Garruncho Beach and the historic Church of Saint Mary in Muros.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is well-equipped with amenities. You'll find benches, tables in small recreational areas, and fountains. There are also covered sections for shelter. The towns of Muros de Nalón and San Esteban de Pravia, which the route connects, offer cafes, pubs, and restrooms.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of the Great Route GR-204 and integrates with the Northern Route of the Camino de Santiago. It also includes sections of the Paseo de los Miradores and the Senda Espíritu Santo a San Esteban.

faqs.question: Is there a strategic way to tackle the 420 steps on this route?
faqs.answer: The 420 steps from the port of San Esteban up to the Holy Spirit Viewpoint are considered the most strenuous part. If you prefer to descend these steps rather than climb them, consider starting your hike from the Muros de Nalón side and heading towards San Esteban, then returning. This way, you'll tackle the steps going down.

waypoints.name: Church of Saint Mary in Muros
